construct_description: The ability to explore, interpret, and draw sound conclusions from emergency medical services performance data — including recognizing patterns in response times and patient outcomes, questioning operational root causes, and translating analysis into actionable recommendations for EMS system improvement.

pba_category.name: Process/Demonstration Assessment
pba_category.description: Tasks that assess the process of problem-solving, not just the final answer. The system observes and logs how you investigate.

dimensions.name: Exploration
dimensions.description: Do you examine the data broadly before jumping to conclusions?

dimensions.name: Pattern Recognition
dimensions.description: Can you identify trends, outliers, and meaningful patterns across units and quarters?

dimensions.name: Hypothesis Testing
dimensions.description: Do you test your ideas against the data rather than assuming?

dimensions.name: Interpretation & Recommendation
dimensions.description: Can you translate findings into clear, evidence-backed operational recommendations?

dataset.name: Tri-County EMS — Unit Performance Report Q1–Q4
dataset.description: Response time and cardiac arrest survival data across 4 EMS district units. Average response times spiked in one district in Q3 — figure out which unit is underperforming, why survival rates correlate, and what to recommend.
insight_hints: Unit B — Riverside had a dramatic Q3 spike: avg response time jumped from ~7.5 to 11.4 min
Unit B Q3 availability dropped to 71% — the lowest of any unit-quarter in the dataset
Unit B Q3 crew hours dropped from ~1344 to 1008 — suggesting a staffing reduction
Cardiac arrest survival in Unit B Q3 fell to 18% — lowest in the dataset
Response time and survival rate are strongly inversely correlated across the dataset
Unit D Highland Rural has persistently high response times — a structural geographic challenge, not a Q3 anomaly
Unit A Metro North is the best performer on response time and cardiac survival
Unit B recovered in Q4 — both response time and survival rate returned to near-baseline

task.title: Tri-County EMS — Q3 Performance Review
task.briefing:
Tri-County Emergency Medical Services is reviewing Q3 performance across its four district units ahead of a county board presentation. Leadership has flagged that something happened in Q3 that affected response times and patient outcomes — but they don't know which unit, why, or how serious the impact was.

Your job: explore the unit performance data, identify what went wrong and where, and write a brief recommendation for the board. Be specific — use the data.
task.writing_prompt: Based on your analysis, write a 150–250 word memo to Tri-County EMS leadership identifying: (1) which unit had the most significant Q3 performance issue, (2) what the data suggests caused it, (3) the patient outcome impact, and (4) your recommended corrective action.
